course_description: This course focuses on the management of ethical speech pathology services, principles of leadership, and professional business skills for working in the private sector. Students will learn through direct teaching and mock planning situations.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims: According to figures released by Speech Pathology Australia, well over half of speech pathologists working in Australia are working in private practice. This course exposes students to the requirements of working in business, private practice and as a speech pathology service provider for the National Disability Insurance Scheme. Throughout the course, students will independently engage in online learning resources, and participate in weekly tutorials applying authentic case-based learning activities. In the tutorials, students will actively participate in independent, small group and large group learning activities. Although this provides students with more flexibility to learn, the associated workload is similar to that of a traditional course. In this course, the students will gain understanding of leadership qualities in speech pathology, the value of supervision and mentoring, networking in a professional capacity, advocacy, the professional, business and ethical responsibilities of working in private practice alongside further develop their professional clinical reasoning skills.

learning_outcomes.description: Engage critically and constructively with the professional, legal and ethical responsibilities and requirements of working in private practice. SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.2, 1.3; SPA Code of Ethics

Generate creative and practical solutions to promote private practice engagement for speech pathology stakeholders. Speech Pathology Australia Professional Standards (2020) (SPA PS) Domains/Standards 1.3, 2.2, 2.5, 2.7, 3.6

Advocate for and work in professional and interprofessional teams to support private practice as a health professional to support continued practice within the NDIS and the community more broadly. SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1 – 1.7, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1 – 3.6
Describe the value of supervision, mentoring and networking in a private practice SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.3, 2.2; SPA Code of Ethics.
Explain business and marketing obligations for contemporary private practice. SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5; SPA Code of Ethics

Understand the influences on, and mechanisms by which, speech pathologists can support and advocate effective and ethical management of NDIS clients within private practice. SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6, 1.7, 2.2, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1

Describe qualities of leadership in speech pathology and critically reflect on personal leadership strengths and opportunities to develop the skills required to become an effective leader. SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5
Reflect on the skills and knowledge required to optimise personal and professional wellbeing within private practice. SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.2, 1.3, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3

assessment_details.title: Case Based Wellness Assignment
assessment_details.due_date: 20/03/2026 1:00 pm A detailed task description and marking criteria will be made available on the course Blackboard site.
assessment_details.task_description: Each student is required to complete an assignment discussing the management of personal and professional wellbeing as a speech pathologist within private practice. With reference to the supplied case information and based on Speech Pathology Australia's Code of Ethics and Professional Standards, students will discuss the speech pathologist's risks for burnout, how to manage wellness, and reflect on their own practice and wellness management in relation to the case. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The assessment task is to be submitted via the course e-learning Blackboard site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Extensions can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for an extension request via my.UQ > my.UQ Dashboard > my. Requests and provide evidence of circumstance, as soon as it becomes evident that an extension is needed, but no later than the assessment item submission date. Further information regarding what constitutes an acceptable reason for an extension is listed on the my.UQ website: Applying for an extension - my.UQ - University of Queensland
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: In-Semester Exam
assessment_details.due_date: 1/04/2026 2:00 pm The exam will be held during class time
assessment_details.task_description: Using the Speech Pathology Australia code of ethics, professional standards and other relevant SPA policies and guidelines, students will be asked discuss content from private practice scenarios and apply content learnt in this course. This examination will be an in-person on-campus School based In-semester examination via Inspera . Students will have access to the Speech Pathology Australia Code of Ethics and Professional Standards during the examination. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Private Practice Resource and Presentation
assessment_details.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Presentation, Product/ Design
assessment_details.due_date: 17/04/2026 1:00 pm 16/06/2026 1:00 pm 16/06/2026 5:00 pm A detailed task description and marking criteria will be made available on the course Blackboard site.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Working in groups with a private practice partner, students will develop a resource that has been determined by the private practice. This resource will be co-designed with the private practice partner to support professional conduct, life-long learning, and the practice of new graduate speech pathologists in this sector. The resource will be showcased in a recorded presentation. Students will also develop a business plan advocating for the resource they are developing. This business plan will advocate for the purpose and efficacy of the resource within the private practice. Students will also complete a peer assessment on teamwork. This assessment has three (3) different components: Component 1: Business Plan Due Date: 17/04/2026 1:00 pm Weight: 15% Description: Students will provide a business plan for their private practice resource Submission: Component 1 will be submitted via the course eLearning Blackboard site. An SHRS coversheet signed by the student must accompany the submission. Component 2: Private Practice Resource and Presentation Due Date: 16/06/2026 1:00 pm Weight: 30% Description: Students will provide a copy of their private practice resource and a recorded presentation discussing the resource. Submission: Component 2 will be submitted via the course eLearning Blackboard site. An SHRS coversheet signed by the student must accompany the submission. Component 3: Peer Evaluation (Buddycheck) Due Date: 16/06/2026 5:00 pm Weight: 5% Description: Students will complete a peer assessment on teamwork Submission: Component 3 will be submitted via the course eLearning Blackboard site. Further details will be made available on the SPCH4111 Blackboard site. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: SHRS Private Practice Module Quiz
assessment_details.task_description: The School of Health and Rehabilitation has produced private practice modules for student learning. As part of this course (SPCH4111), students will complete module 2 to complement their learning about speech pathology and the private sector. To successfully complete this module, students must complete a small quiz at the end of this module. The quiz does not count towards your overall grade for SPCH4111. Multiple attempts of the quiz are allowed until the passing mark of 80% is achieved. This quiz will be open from 23/2/2026 to 06/03/2026. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students will be required to access and complete the module and quiz via the eLearning Blackboard site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 7 (23 Feb - 19 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Online self-directed learning modules (self-directed learning) A series of weekly online modules will provide information about working as a speech pathologist in the private sector. The self-directed learning module content will cover a broad range of topics, including: Working in the private sector as a speech pathologist Marketing and business skills needed to work in private practice Professional and personal wellbeing in private practice Supervision, mentoring and networking in private practice Key stakeholders in private practice Speech pathologists as leaders, clinicians and entrepreneurs

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 7 (23 Feb - 19 Apr)
learning_activities.topic: Weekly on campus tutorials Tutorials will be conducted from weeks 1-7 of semester and will incorporate applied learning independently, in small group and large group discussions to further explore private practice and speech pathology. Tutorials have been designed for experiential learning and therefore, students are expected to attend and actively participate in all tutorials. It is essential that students prepare for tutorials by completing the online self-paced learning resources and associated preparation activities. As students will be working in groups during this learning activity, tutorials will not be recorded.
